title: conostylis aculeata subsp. septentrionora
intro: Conostylis aculeata subsp. septentrionora, commonly known as the northern prickly conostylis, is a plant species found in Western Australia, characterized by its prickly stems and white or pink flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Conostylis aculeata subsp. septentrionora include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and supporting the immune system. Conostylis aculeata subsp. septentrionora is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or infusions in herbal teas, and its extracts are also used in topical applications and tinctures.
